title: Workplace Discipline
content: **[WORKPLACE DISCIPLINE & DISPUTE RESOLUTION]{.underline}**
**A. Service Overview**

One wrong move in a disciplinary hearing and you're facing a CCMA reinstatement order---even when the employee was clearly guilty . Workplace discipline and dispute resolution in South Africa require strict procedures, fair processes, and solid evidence to protect you from unfair dismissal claims .
**B. Detailed Description**

Disciplinary action isn't just about punishing bad behaviour---it's about following the law . South Africa's Labour Relations Act requires both substantive fairness (the employee is actually guilty) and procedural fairness (you followed the correct process) . Get either wrong and the CCMA will overturn your dismissal, costing you reinstatement, back pay, or compensation .
What we do:

Disciplinary Code & Policy Development -- We create legally compliant disciplinary codes defining workplace rules, expected standards, and progressive disciplinary steps from verbal warnings to dismissal .
Disciplinary Hearing Management -- We chair disciplinary hearings, ensuring procedural fairness, proper evidence presentation, and legally defensible outcomes .
Charge Sheet Drafting -- We draft clear, specific charges that comply with the Code of Good Practice and give employees sufficient notice of allegations .
CCMA Representation -- When disputes escalate to the CCMA, we represent you in conciliation and arbitration, presenting evidence and arguing your case .
Grievance Handling -- We manage employee grievances fairly and promptly, preventing escalation to unfair labour practice disputes .
Workplace Mediation -- We mediate workplace conflicts between employees, or between employees and management, resolving issues before they become formal disputes .
Precautionary Suspension -- We advise on when and how to suspend employees pending investigation, ensuring the suspension is lawful and doesn't trigger unfair labour practice claims .
Investigation Support -- We conduct fair, thorough workplace investigations into misconduct allegations, gathering evidence and interviewing witnesses .
Manager Training -- We train your managers to handle disciplinary matters correctly, chair hearings, apply progressive discipline, and document everything properly .
What you get:
🔹 Legally compliant disciplinary policies and procedures for South Africa
🔹 Fair disciplinary hearings that hold up at the CCMA
🔹 Expert representation when disputes reach the CCMA or Labour Court
🔹 Quick, effective grievance resolution preventing costly escalation
🔹 Trained managers who know how to handle discipline legally
🔹 Protection from unfair dismissal claims and unfair labour practice disputes
**C. Who Is This For?**
✅ Businesses in Gauteng and across South Africa facing misconduct and unsure how to handle it legally
✅ Companies that have dismissed employees and now face CCMA unfair dismissal claims
✅ Employers with no formal disciplinary code or outdated disciplinary procedures
✅ Managers struggling to chair disciplinary hearings fairly and confidently
✅ Organizations dealing with serious misconduct like theft, assault, insubordination, or dishonesty
✅ Private schools managing educator discipline and SACE reporting requirements
✅ Businesses with workplace conflicts, employee grievances, or toxic workplace issues
✅ Companies needing CCMA representation for conciliation or arbitration
**D. Why Choose OptiHR for This Service?**
🚨 Legal authority you can trust -- Our owner is an admitted attorney with Labour Court rights. We don't just advise on discipline---we represent you in the CCMA and Labour Court .
🚨 We chair hearings, not just advise -- Unlike most HR consultants, we act as independent chairpersons for your disciplinary hearings, ensuring procedural fairness and legally defensible outcomes .
🚨 We know what the CCMA looks for -- We've represented employers and employees at the CCMA. We know exactly what commissioners scrutinize and how to build bulletproof cases .
🚨 We handle the tough cases -- Theft, fraud, assault, gross insubordination---we manage high-stakes disciplinary matters where the consequences of getting it wrong are severe .
🚨 We resolve disputes before they escalate -- Through mediation and fair grievance handling, we solve workplace conflicts early, saving you time, money, and CCMA referrals .
🚨 We make discipline practical -- We don't give you complicated policies you'll never use. We build systems that work in the real world and train your managers to use them .
**E. Case Study or Testimonial**
The Dismissal That Cost R180,000---Until We Fixed It

A small manufacturing company in Gauteng dismissed an employee for repeated late-coming. The employee had been warned verbally multiple times, but nothing was documented. When he was finally dismissed, he took the case to the CCMA .
The CCMA commissioner asked three questions:
1\. Did the employee know the rule about punctuality?
2\. Was he given written warnings with clear consequences?
3\. Was he given an opportunity to respond before dismissal?
The employer couldn't prove any of it. The dismissal was declared unfair. The company faced an order for 12 months' compensation---R180,000 .
That's when they called OptiHR. We reviewed their disciplinary policies (they didn't have any), trained their managers, and drafted a compliant disciplinary code .
Six months later, another employee committed serious misconduct---theft of company property. This time, we managed the process:
✔ We conducted a fair investigation and gathered evidence
✔ We drafted clear charges and served them with 5 days' notice
✔ We chaired the disciplinary hearing, giving the employee a chance to respond
✔ We documented everything---evidence, testimony, and the rationale for dismissal
✔ The employee took it to the CCMA. We represented the employer
The result: The CCMA upheld the dismissal. The employer won because the process was fair, documented, and legally sound .

"The first dismissal cost us R180,000 because we didn't know the rules. OptiHR taught us how to do it right. The second dismissal was upheld at the CCMA because the process was fair. That's the difference between guessing and knowing." -- Owner, Manufacturing Company, Gauteng
**F. Compliance & Legal Considerations**
Workplace discipline and dispute resolution must comply with South African labour laws :

📌 Labour Relations Act (LRA), Section 185 -- Employees have the right not to be unfairly dismissed or subjected to unfair labour practices. Unfair suspension or disciplinary action (even if not dismissal) can be challenged at the CCMA .
📌 Labour Relations Act (LRA), Section 186(2) -- Unfair labour practice includes unfair suspension or any unfair disciplinary action short of dismissal .
📌 Code of Good Practice: Dismissal (Schedule 8) -- Requires employers to set clear rules, communicate them to employees, apply them consistently, and follow fair procedures before dismissing .
📌 Substantive fairness -- The employee must actually be guilty of the offence. You must prove it with evidence .
📌 Procedural fairness -- You must follow the correct process: investigation, charge sheet, notice, hearing, opportunity to respond, representation rights, and a fair sanction .

📌 Progressive discipline -- Except for serious misconduct (theft, assault, gross dishonesty, gross insubordination), you must apply progressive discipline: verbal warning → written warning → final written warning → dismissal .
📌 Notice periods -- Employees must receive at least 5 working days' notice before a disciplinary hearing .
📌 Right to representation -- Employees have the right to be represented by a fellow employee or shop steward (if there's a recognized union) .
📌 Written reasons -- Employees must be given written reasons for disciplinary decisions .
📌 Right to appeal -- Employees must be given the right to appeal disciplinary outcomes .
📌 Warning validity periods -- Written warnings typically remain valid for 3-6 months; final written warnings for 12 months .
📌 CCMA referral deadlines -- Employees have 30 days from date of dismissal to refer unfair dismissal disputes to the CCMA .

**H. FAQ (1--3 for each service)**
Q1: Can we dismiss someone immediately for serious misconduct?

Yes, but only if the misconduct is serious enough (theft, assault, gross dishonesty, gross insubordination) and you still follow a fair process . You must investigate, serve charges, hold a hearing, give the employee a chance to respond, and document the decision . "Immediate" doesn't mean "skip the process"---it means you don't need progressive warnings first .
Q2: What happens if we dismiss someone without a hearing?

The CCMA will declare the dismissal procedurally unfair, even if the employee was guilty . You'll face reinstatement (the employee comes back) or compensation (up to 12 months' salary for unfair dismissal) . South African labour law requires a fair hearing before dismissal---no exceptions .
Q3: How long do warnings stay valid?

Written warnings typically remain valid for 3-6 months, and final written warnings for 12 months . After that period, they expire and can't be used for progressive discipline. But this depends on your disciplinary policy---we help you set appropriate validity periods for your business .
**I. Call-to-Action**
Don't let one unfair dismissal cost you months of salary in compensation.
Workplace discipline and dispute resolution done right protect you from CCMA claims, keep your workplace functional, and give you the confidence to manage misconduct fairly and legally .
👉 Request Your Free Disciplinary Consultation and find out if your disciplinary processes will hold up at the CCMA---before you find out the hard way.
